title: Executive Order 10460—Providing for the Performance by the Director of Defense Mobilization of Certain Functions Relating to Telecommunications

flags.justification: Frequency-assignment authority operates under an existing statutory framework (Communications Act §305), and Sec. 4 explicitly preserves the FCC's existing authority and jurisdiction — a bounded, coordination-focused grant, not a broad new concentration.
Sec. 3's Interdepartmental Radio Advisory Committee reporting function and Sec. 4's explicit FCC-cooperation requirement provide real structural checks.
Single coherent purpose: telecommunications policy coordination.
Functions are specifically enumerated (five named areas) with reference to an existing statutory provision for frequency assignment.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.

Standing-procedural-framework category applies: this is an ongoing, evergreen administrative coordination function, and the order itself demonstrates a pattern of periodic reorganization by superseding a prior similar arrangement (revoking EO 10297).
Sec. 4 explicitly protects the FCC's existing jurisdiction — the opposite of removing oversight.
The FCC-cooperation requirement and Advisory Committee input mechanism provide real external checks.
A routine, bounded administrative coordination assignment, explicitly limited by preserving the FCC's separate authority.

The explicit FCC-authority-preservation clause (Sec. 4) is precisely the kind of anti-conflict safeguard this flag looks for; Sec. 5's transfer from the prior Telecommunications Advisor role is a deliberate, clean succession.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/20 = 0.0%. A well-designed coordination order with an explicit, textually clear FCC-authority preservation clause.
